title: phlox idahonis
intro: Phlox idahonis, also known as Idaho phlox or Western phlox, is a flowering plant species native to the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits include reducing fever, treating sore throats, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and colds. Idaho phlox can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies for its soothing and protective effects.
